column1: # Downstream regression manifest — tish CONSUMERS built/tested against tish HEAD.
# Columns (TAB-separated): name  source  subdir  kind  cmd  expected
#   source:   git:URL@REF | local:PATH (dev machine only) | self:SUBDIR (a subdir of the tish checkout)
#   kind:     rust (cargo: extension/embedder) | ffi (extern-C extension) | tish (runs .tish programs)
#   expected: pass  = must build+test clean (a failure is a REGRESSION → fails the suite)
#             xfail = known-broken on this branch pending migration (green now; flags if it gets FIXED)
# For `tish` kind the runner builds the HEAD binary, `npm install`s the repo, and overwrites the
# bundled @tishlang/tish binary with HEAD so the repo's OWN test/build harness runs against HEAD.
# Run:  regression/downstream/run.sh all          # local: everything below
#       regression/downstream/run.sh --git-only   # CI: only git:/self: sources (local: skipped)
# ── ffi: extern-C extensions — the C-ABI is stable, so these MUST keep working ──
ffi-mathext
ffi-statext
tish-unity
# ── cargo: Rust extensions/embedders — broken by NativeFn→Callable / String→ArcStr / PropMap ──
# (CONFIRMED broken by compiling against feature/perf: tish-pg, tish-callbacks. tish-apple-common
# is CONFIRMED CLEAN — it is in the tish-program section below as pass; its tish-macos/tish-ios
# crates are macOS-only + heavy Value-use, likely broken, but not Linux-checkable.)
tish-pg
tish-callbacks
tish-oidc
tish-stripe
tish-cedar
tish-biscuit
tish-webauthn
brainstorm-pg
tish-polars
tish-apple
# ── tish-program consumers — no compile link; hit only by SEMANTIC changes (div-by-zero, key order) ──
# Tested via the repo's own build/test harness against the HEAD binary (see header). expected=pass —
# the language surface only GREW, so these should keep working; a RED means a real break OR a cmd that
# needs per-repo tuning (entry/feature flags). Refine the cmd as you wire each repo.
lattish
tish-ide-panels
# tish-audio: a lattish APP. Builds every step against HEAD (css + esbuild bridge + 12 worklets via the
# HEAD tish binary) and fails ONLY at `import {createRoot} from "lattish"` — lattish isn't resolvable in
# an isolated clone (its npm package name is still being fixed; see lattish's @npm-package-name-fix ref).
# NOT a HEAD break → xfail until the harness provisions lattish as a sibling/node_modules package.
tish-audio
tish-learn
tish-playground
# tish-midi (deckard): a lattish APP. Its own npm scripts are monorepo-bound (`cd ../tish && cargo run`,
# no `test` script) so they can't run under the copy-to-workdir model. We instead transpile-smoke its
# JS-targetable `src/` (excluding `services/`, which are native-module `tish run` daemons). Currently
# xfails on `import ... from "lattish"` (same lattish-resolution gap as tish-audio) — NOT a HEAD break.
tish-midi
column5: bash -c 'rc=0; while IFS= read -r f; do tish build --target js "$f" -o /tmp/_m.js >/tmp/_m.err 2>&1 || { echo "FAIL: $f"; head -2 /tmp/_m.err; rc=1; }; done < <(find src -name "*.tish" -not -path "*/node_modules/*"); exit $rc'
